title: Causal-Audit: Explicit and Auditable Graph-based Reasoning via Target-Aware Causal Chain Construction
authors: Su Lan; Xuefei Yin; Yanming Zhu; Alan Wee-Chung Liew
venue: Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: ACL 2026
conference_title: Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: ACL 2026
abstract: Causal and intervention-based question answering is fundamental to advancing large language models toward reasoning beyond surface-level correlations and understanding underlying causal mechanisms. However, existing language model methods often rely on implicit language-level reasoning, which leads to opaque causal assumptions, unverifiable reasoning paths, and fragile predictions under complex interventions. This work proposes an explicit and auditable graph-based reasoning framework with target-aware causal graph construction and path-level causal evidence aggregation.
citation_text: Su Lan, Xuefei Yin, Yanming Zhu, and Alan Wee-Chung Liew. "Causal-Audit: Explicit and Auditable Graph-based Reasoning via Target-Aware Causal Chain Construction." Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: ACL 2026.

title: PowerFDNet: Deep Learning-Based Stealthy False Data Injection Attack Detection for AC-model Transmission Systems
authors: Xuefei Yin; Yanming Zhu; Yiyu Xie; Jun Hu
venue: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
journal_title: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning based method for detecting stealthy false data injection attacks in AC-model transmission systems. The method is designed to improve the robustness and practical utility of attack detection under realistic power system settings.
citation_text: X. Yin, Y. Zhu, Y. Xie and J. Hu, "PowerFDNet: Deep Learning-Based Stealthy False Data Injection Attack Detection for AC-model Transmission Systems," IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society, vol. 3, pp. 149-161, 2022.

title: 3D Fingerprint
authors: Yanming Zhu; Wenxiu Zhou; Xuefei Yin; Jun Hu
venue: Encyclopedia of Cryptography, Security and Privacy
abstract: An overview of 3D fingerprint representation and analysis, covering core concepts, acquisition considerations, and security relevance in biometric systems.
citation_text: Zhu, Y., Zhou, W., Yin, X., and Hu, J. (2021). 3D Fingerprint. Encyclopedia of Cryptography, Security and Privacy. Springer.
